t_ch: 过节？没有没有，她可是下层区唯一一个良心医生。
t_en: A dispute? No, no, she's the only kindhearted doctor in the Underworld.

t_ch: 我只是不太认可她的做法，缺乏效率，而且太过理想主义……
t_en: I just don't really approve of her methods, that's all. Too idealistic, and no real efficacy...

t_ch: 我也早看她不爽了——
t_en: I'm through seeing her too...

t_ch: 啊？原来和她有过节的是你啊？
t_en: Huh? So you had a dispute with her?

t_ch: …她可是下层区唯一一个良心医生。我只是不太认可她的做法，缺乏效率，而且太过理想主义……
t_en: ...She's the only kindhearted doctor in the Underworld. I just don't really approve of her methods, that's all. Too idealistic, and no real efficacy...

t_ch: 什么人都想救，就意味着谁也救不了——到头来只是折磨了患者，又累倒了自己。
t_en: She tries to save everyone, which means she can't save anyone... In the end, it's the patients who suffer, and she just tires herself out.

t_ch: 像我这样的矿工，根本等不起什么「漫长的疗程」——吃了药以后要是不能立刻返工赚钱，那和晾着等死差别也不大……
t_en: Miners like me don't have time for lengthy treatments... If I can't take something and get right back to work, I may as well lie down and wait for death to come knocking...

t_ch: 你知道瓦赫医生吗？他做的特效药不仅便宜，还能立刻起效，只需要承担一点点风险……
t_en: Did you know Dr. Vache? His wonder medicine is cheap and the results are immediate. You just need to be willing to... take a little risk.

t_ch: 「风险」？
t_en: "Risk"?

t_ch: 瓦赫医生的药很特殊，有很多人在服用之后痊愈，也有一些人在服药之后病情加剧，不幸离世——
t_en: Dr. Vache's medicine is... different. Lots of people make a full recovery after taking it... but some get worse, and the unlucky ones check out early.

t_ch: 不过就算知道有「风险」，大家还是争着抢着想拿到这种药，你知道为什么吗？
t_en: Even knowing the risk, most people fought tooth and nail to get a hold of the medicine. You know why?

t_ch: 他们宁愿拿生命赌博…
t_en: They're willing to gamble with their life...

t_ch: 哼，你还挺懂道理的嘛？
t_en: Hmph, you're smarter than you look.

t_ch: 我无法理解。
t_en: I've no idea.

t_ch: 哼…因为你没有经历过那种穷困潦倒啊。
t_en: Hmph... you've clearly never been in really dire straits.

t_ch: 被生活逼迫到那种地步的人，任何放在眼前的机会都不会放过…哪怕是要他们拿命去赌。
t_en: When life forces you to the edge, you'll throw yourself at any opportunity... even if it means risking it all.

t_ch: 对于我们这种人，缓慢又痛苦的疗程跟被宣判死刑也没什么两样。与其说服我们去受那种折磨…还不如来个干脆痛快的。
t_en: For people like us, a slow and tortuous treatment is the same as a death sentence. If it's between that and a quick result, we'll take the latter.

t_ch: 咳，算了，跟你说那么多干嘛？反正瓦赫医生已经不在了……
t_en: Ahem, forget it. Why am I telling you all this? Dr. Vache is dead, anyway...

t_ch: 该去哪里弄这种「特效药」呢？
t_en: Where can I go to get this wonder medicine?

t_ch: 如果我说，我认同你的想法…
t_en: What if I told you I agreed with you?

t_ch: 哈，怎么，你也对「特效药」起兴趣了？
t_en: Hah, what, now you're interested in the wonder medicine?

t_ch: 偷偷告诉你吧，「疑难重症患者互助会」现在还在卖这种药…让我告诉你他们的交易地点也不是不行，但你得遵守一条规矩……
t_en: Listen up... the Complex Illness Mutual Aid Society is still selling that medicine... I can tell you where they trade, but there's a rule...

t_ch: ——一定不能把这些信息告诉娜塔莎医生，明白了吗？你要是敢违反这条规矩，后果自负！
t_en: ...You must not tell any of this to Dr. Natasha, you hear? If you break the rule, you'll face the consequences!

t_ch: 喏，拿着吧，这是「疑难重症患者互助会」卖药的地址。我再最后强调一遍——不许把这消息透露给娜塔莎！
t_en: Here, take this, it's the address. A final warning — do not reveal this information to Natasha!

t_ch: （拿到卖「特效药」的地址了，接下来该怎么做呢…）
t_en: (I know where to find the wonder medicine. What should I do next...?)

t_ch: （那个患者不让我把这件事告诉娜塔莎，该不该听他的呢？）
t_en: (That patient didn't want me to tell Natasha about this... Should I listen to him?)

t_ch: 先去告诉娜塔莎吧。
t_en: Let's tell Natasha first.

t_ch: 先去调查一下卖药的地址吧。
t_en: Let's investigate the wonder medicine address first.

t_ch: 解释「互助会」的情报
t_en: Explain what you found out about the mutual aid society.

t_ch: 也就是说，「互助会」找到了哥…瓦赫留下的研究成果，而且还在向一些患者出售那些所谓的「特效药」……
t_en: So the mutual aid society found my bro— ...Vache's research results... and now they're selling this so-called "wonder medicine" to patients...?

t_ch: 能做出这种事…足以证明他们不是什么纯粹的「互助会」。
t_en: Any organization doing something like that is interested in more than just "mutual aid..."

t_ch: 患者们还希望我对你保密。
t_en: The patients wanted me to keep it a secret from you.

t_ch: 这也难怪。我坚决反对他们服用所谓的「特效药」，他们肯定也知道我的态度。
t_en: I'm not surprised. They'll know that I'm completely against them using this "wonder medicine..."

t_ch: 患者说愿意承受风险。
t_en: The patient said he was willing to take the risk.

t_ch: …作为对他们的生命负责的医生，我不能接受这种说辞。我对所谓「特效药」的态度不会改变。
t_en: ...As a doctor, I don't accept that — I'm responsible for their well-being. My attitude towards this "wonder medicine" won't change.

t_ch: 瓦赫留下的那些药物…不对，那些甚至不能算「药物」，只是些实验中产生的副产品、试剂罢了。
t_en: What Vache left behind is nothing more than reagents and experimental by-products... The word "medicine" doesn't apply here.

t_ch: 我无数次警告过那些患者，但他们还是执迷不悟…也怪不得他们不愿意把和「互助会」有关的消息透露给我。
t_en: I warned the patients countless times, but it seems like they had their heads in the sand all along... No wonder they kept the mutual aid society a secret from me.

t_ch: 谢谢你，{NICKNAME}。即使那些人说了很多于我不利的话，你还是对我如此信任。
t_en: Thank you, {NICKNAME}. Those patients weren't exactly singing my praises — thank you for having faith in me.

t_ch: 你要是还好奇瓦赫那所谓「特效药」的效用，不如去问问诊所里的患者吧——看看他们会给你什么样的答案。
t_en: If you're curious about Vache's wonder medicine, you should ask some of the patients in the clinic... see what they have to say about the effects.

t_ch: 站住，你这是要去哪？
t_en: Hey, where do you think you're going?

t_ch: 去找「互助会」买药。
t_en: To buy medicine from the mutual aid society.

t_ch: 买药？哦…明白了，你也是奔着瓦赫医生的特效药来的，是吧？
t_en: Medicine? Oh... you mean Dr. Vache's wonder medicine, right?

t_ch: 只是散步经过…
t_en: Just taking a walk...

t_ch: 到裂界里散步吗？哼，别装了。
t_en: In the Fragmentum? Hmph, gimme a break.

t_ch: 路过这儿的穷酸流浪汉都跟你一个样——想买药的话直接跟我说就行，没必要再往前走了。
t_en: You're the same as everyone else that comes through here. If you want medicine, you don't need to go any further — you talk to me.

t_ch: 哦，对了，这里只收现金，概不赊账……
t_en: And we only take cash, this ain't a charity...

t_ch: 我听说这儿的药很便宜。
t_en: I heard the medicine here is pretty cheap.

t_ch: 价是「互助会」后来定的，虽然不像瓦赫医生那样接近免费送，但也比诊所那女人卖的药便宜不少。
t_en: The mutual aid society sets the price. It might be different from Dr. Vache and his handouts, but it's a darn sight cheaper than that clinic woman's medicine.

t_ch: …我说，你到底买不买，能不能给个痛快话？我没空跟你扯闲天。
t_en: ...Well, are you buying or not? Spit it out. I don't have time for chit-chat.

t_ch: 万一病人手上没钱呢？
t_en: What if a patient doesn't have the money?

t_ch: 没钱？没钱还买什么药啊，自己乖乖回家等死不行吗？
t_en: No money? Then you don't get any. You go home and wait for death.

t_ch: …我说，你是不是在试探我啊？没钱就快滚开，别耽误我卖药。
t_en: ...Say, what are you trying to pull here? If you can't afford it, then get outta my sight. I've got medicine to sell.

t_ch: 给我来一个疗程吧。
t_en: I'll take a course of treatment.

t_ch: 哼，下次跟人做生意，记得少说点废话…我的时间宝贵得很。
t_en: Hmph, cut the small talk next time... my time is valuable.

t_ch: 你态度不能放好点吗？
t_en: Must be difficult being this unpleasant.

t_ch: 怎么，爱买不买，你还能拿我怎么着不成？
t_en: Huh? What are you gonna do about it? Either buy something or clear out.

t_ch: 你们不该为穷人着想的吗？
t_en: Shouldn't you think about those in need?

t_ch: 那是瓦赫说的话，跟我们「互助会」没关系。
t_en: You sound like Vache. That isn't how the mutual aid society works.

t_ch: 等会儿，你这身打扮…你是那个女医生的熟人吧？我之前去诊所的时候瞥见过你，肯定没错！
t_en: Wait a minute, your clothes... Are you friends with that doctor woman? I've seen you at the clinic before — I know it!

t_ch: 走开，走开！离远点，我这儿不欢迎和那个女人有瓜葛的家伙！今天药店关门了，谁也别来烦我！
t_en: Get lost — now! If you're a friend of that woman then you're not welcome here! Shop's closed today, no customers!

t_ch: 等…等一下！那我们的药怎么办？我们都等了这么久了……
t_en: ...W—Wait! What about our medicine? We've been waiting for ages...

t_ch: 散开，都散开！别废话了，你们改天再来吧！
t_en: Get lost! All of ya! Save your breath and come back some other time!

t_ch: …都、都怪你，害得我们买不上特效药了！
t_en: ...Th—This is all your fault! How are we supposed to get our wonder medicine now!?

t_ch: 你快走吧，你在这儿待着，怕是那家伙永远都不会再营业了……
t_en: You should leave — if you stay here that guy might not ever sell to us again...

t_ch: （那个卖药的家伙，好像表现得特别心虚……）
t_en: (That medicine seller sure shut up shop in a hurry...)

t_ch: （果然还是回去向娜塔莎汇报一下吧。）
t_en: (I should head back and report to Natasha.)

t_ch: 解释「互助会」的情报
t_en: Explain what you found out about the mutual aid society.

t_ch: 也就是说，「互助会」找到了哥…瓦赫留下的研究成果，而且还在向一些患者出售那些所谓的「特效药」……
t_en: So the mutual aid society found my bro— ...Vache's research results... and now they're selling this so-called "wonder medicine" to patients...?

t_ch: 能做出这种事…足以证明他们不是什么纯粹的「互助会」。
t_en: Any organization doing something like that is interested in more than just "mutual aid..."

t_ch: 患者们还希望我对你保密。
t_en: The patients wanted me to keep it a secret from you.

t_ch: 这也难怪。我坚决反对他们服用所谓的「特效药」，他们肯定也知道我的态度。
t_en: I'm not surprised. They'll know that I'm completely against them using this "wonder medicine..."

t_ch: 患者说愿意承受风险。
t_en: The patient said he was willing to take the risk.

t_ch: …作为对他们的生命负责的医生，我不能接受这种说辞。我对所谓「特效药」的态度不会改变。
t_en: ...As a doctor, I don't accept that — I'm responsible for their well-being. My attitude towards this "wonder medicine" won't change.

t_ch: 瓦赫留下的那些药物…不对，那些甚至不能算「药物」，只是些实验中产生的副产品、试剂罢了。
t_en: What Vache left behind is nothing more than reagents and experimental by-products... The word "medicine" doesn't apply here.

t_ch: 我无数次警告过那些患者，但他们还是执迷不悟…也怪不得他们不愿意把和「互助会」有关的消息透露给我。
t_en: I warned the patients countless times, but it seems like they had their heads in the sand all along... No wonder they kept the mutual aid society a secret from me.

t_ch: 那些人应该是说了很多于我不利的话，所以你才会先去卖药的窝点一探究竟吧？
t_en: I'm guessing the patients you met weren't exactly singing my praises, which is why you tracked the dealer down and made inquiries first.

t_ch: 我不怪你，{NICKNAME}。感谢你事后还能来找我，这份信任对我来说很重要。
t_en: I don't blame you, {NICKNAME}. After everything you heard, I'm grateful you came back to find me. Your trust is important to me.

t_ch: 你如果好奇瓦赫那所谓「特效药」的效用，不如去问问诊所里的患者吧——看看他们会给你什么样的答案。
t_en: If you're curious about Vache's wonder medicine, you should ask some of the patients in the clinic... see what they have to say about the effects.

t_ch: 你问我瓦赫留下的药？那玩意…真的能称得上是药吗？
t_en: The medicine Vache left behind? That stuff... Can you even call it medicine?

t_ch: 虽然娜塔莎医生也在这里，但我还是得实话实说…依我看，瓦赫欠缺医德，根本算不上是个医生！
t_en: I know Dr. Natasha's here, but I have to tell it like it is... Vache had zero medical ethics — he didn't deserve the title "doctor."

t_ch: 他是有一身医生的本事，但却不肯好好用那本事治病救人——我以前去找他看病的时候，他甚至连病症都不问清楚，就给我开了所谓的「特效药」……
t_en: He may have had the capability, but he didn't use it to help anyone... Whenever I went to see him, he never even asked about my illness! He just prescribed me his so-called "wonder medicine..."

t_ch: 换作是你，那药你敢吃吗？
t_en: If it were you, would you have taken that medicine?

t_ch: 有人说瓦赫医生是主动从上层区下来帮忙的大善人…但我可不这么觉得。
t_en: Some people say that Dr. Vache was a saint who came from the Overworld to help those in need... I don't buy it.

t_ch: 「穷人们的医生」这种说法，纯粹是某些人在自我感动而已。瓦赫以前虽然经常发放一些免费的药物，但他其实压根不关心那些吃药的人……
t_en: "People's doctor..." That's just what people who've bought into the myth like to say. Vache used to distribute free medicine all the time, but, uh... he couldn't have cared less about the people taking the medicine...

t_ch: 说得难听点，他不过是把我们当成了实验对象。病能不能治好不重要，重要的是得有人愿意傻呵呵地当他的小地鼠……
t_en: The hard truth is that we were just his test subjects. It didn't matter if the medicine worked or not. What mattered was having a constant stream of willing lab rats...

t_ch: …当然了，我控制不了别人怎么想，这些也只是我自己的推测。
t_en: ...Of course, I can't control what anyone else thinks. That's just how I see it.

t_ch: …瓦赫的事，我还没想好该怎么向你传达。我们之间的关系…还有他在下层区做过的事，这些旧事都说来话长。
t_en: ...I still haven't worked out how to convey it to you, the issues surrounding Vache. My relationship with him... the things he's done in the Underworld... it's a long story.

t_ch: 关于瓦赫，你应该也已经听到许多不同的意见了。{NICKNAME}，能告诉我你的想法吗？
t_en: I'm guessing you've heard a lot of different opinions on Vache. What do you make of him, {NICKNAME}?

t_ch: 他是个欠缺医德的医生。
t_en: I think he was a doctor with zero medical ethics.

t_ch: 我难以评价。
t_en: It's tough for me to say.

t_ch: 他是不是精神分裂？
t_en: I think he's insane.

t_ch: 你是这么认为的吗？也难怪……
t_en: I see... I can understand that.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，我想让你再去见一位病人，她也是那「特效药」的受害者——我想让你看到滥用药物给患者带来的切实后果。
t_en: {NICKNAME}, I want you to go and visit another patient — a victim of the wonder medicine... I want you to see the real consequences of abusing that medicine.

t_ch: 等你了解了她的情况以后，就再回来找我聊聊看法吧。然后…我想拜托你帮我一个忙。
t_en: Once you've understood her situation, come back and tell me what you think. After that... I want to ask for your help again.

t_ch: 我和患者聊完了。
t_en: I'm finished talking with patients.

t_ch: 就是那位病人了，{NICKNAME}。
t_en: That's the patient, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 和她搭话时要小心，她的精神状态很不稳定…我已经照顾了她很长时间，但始终没能帮她调整到正常状态。
t_en: Watch what you say to her, she's not in the most stable mental state... I've taken care of her for a long time, but still haven't been able to get her back to her old self.

t_ch: 去尝试着和她交流一下吧，{NICKNAME}。尽量表现得有亲和力一些…不要吓到病人了。
t_en: Try talking with her, {NICKNAME}. And try to be as kind as possible... you don't want to scare her.

t_ch: …你是谁……
t_en: ...Who are you...?

t_ch: 我是娜塔莎医生的朋友。
t_en: I'm a friend of Dr. Natasha.

t_ch: 我是…从天外降临的神医。
t_en: I'm a... miracle doctor from beyond the sky.

t_ch: …谁？我又是…谁？
t_en: ...Who? And... who am I?

t_ch: 您还没自我介绍。
t_en: You haven't introduced yourself yet.

t_ch: 您是娜塔莎的病人，记得吗？
t_en: You're one of Dr. Natasha's patients, don't you remember?

t_ch: …我老公在哪里，我的孩子又在哪里……
t_en: ...Where's my husband... and where's my child...?

t_ch: …他们在哪里？你说…你知道对不对？
t_en: ...Wh—Where are they? You... you know where they are, right?

t_ch: 你还好吗？我真的不知道…
t_en: I'm afraid not. Are you okay?

t_ch: 请您冷静一下…
t_en: Try and stay calm...

t_ch: …你不知道…她也不知道…大家都不知道……
t_en: ...You don't know... they don't know... nobody knows...

t_ch: 到哪去了…他们…到哪去了……
t_en: Where... Wh—Where did they go...

t_ch: 真可怜啊，那个女人…她的丈夫孩子都生病去世了。
t_en: That poor, poor lady... Both her husband and child became ill and passed away.

t_ch: 他们之前病得很严重吗？
t_en: They must have been seriously ill...

t_ch: 哎…也说不上多严重，只是偶尔需要卧床治疗，耽误了工作生计。
t_en: *sigh* ...Not terribly serious. But they needed to get treatment every now and then, which did impact their livelihood.

t_ch: 她会变成这样就是这个原因吗？
t_en: And that made her become like this?

t_ch: 是啊…在家人去世之前，她还是挺开朗的一个人。
t_en: Yes... Before her family passed away, she was a carefree young woman.

t_ch: 她的老公和孩子都得了一种怪病，虽说症状不算太严重，但工作生计难免会受些影响。
t_en: Her husband and child caught a mysterious illness. The symptoms weren't grave, but their livelihood was certainly impacted.

t_ch: 为了能一次性彻底治愈，他们就去买那什么「特效药」…谁能想到，改用了那药之后，两人的病情立马变得更严重了。
t_en: In order to try and rid themselves of the ailment once and for all, they bought some kind of "wonder medicine..." Who would've thought their condition would deteriorate so rapidly after they began using it.

t_ch: 那副遭罪的模样，简直生不如死…过了没多久，两个人就前后脚离开了。
t_en: The two began to suffer terribly, such that death became a blessing... It wasn't long before they departed this world, one after the other.

t_ch: 那之后她就一直是这种状态。多亏娜塔莎医生主动帮她调养，否则的话…哎，真不敢想。
t_en: From that moment on, oh she became as you see her now. If Dr. Natasha hadn't taken her under her wing... *sigh* Well it doesn't bear thinking about.

t_ch: 这就是纯粹的毒药吧…
t_en: It just sounds like poison...

t_ch: 那能怎么办呢？卖药的事先也都警告过了，说这疗法有风险，出了情况只能赖自己运气太差……
t_en: And yet what more could've been done? They were warned about the medicine. They were told about the risks... They chose to roll the dice.

t_ch: 也许真的和运气太差有关…
t_en: They were unlucky...

t_ch: 你真这么觉得吗？在我看来啊，那些卖药的就是在明目张胆地谋财害命啊…怎么能把这种东西卖给生病的人呢？
t_en: Just unlucky? As far as I'm concerned, those medicine dealers are making money for murder in broad daylight... Who would sell such a thing to the sick?

t_ch: 哎，多的话我也没力气说了。至少现在我和老伴儿还能尽力帮帮她，希望她有朝一日还能像个正常人一样生活吧……
t_en: *sigh* Talking about it tires me out. At least my husband and I are able to help her where we can. I just hope that one day she can lead a normal life again...

t_ch: 年轻人，来这边吧，别打扰那个可怜人了…我跟你说几句……
t_en: Come over here, child. It's best to leave that poor lady in peace. Let me explain...

t_ch: 你问我她怎么了？这已经够明显了吧，先是遭遇了医疗事故，然后精神上又受了打击……
t_en: What's wrong with her? Isn't it obvious? Her family were the victims of improper medical treatment, then she had a nervous breakdown...

t_ch: 真是无可救药啊，我这蠢妹妹…哎……
t_en: *sigh* Oh sister, sometimes there just isn't a cure...

t_ch: 那个叫瓦赫的医生，简直就不是人…是披着大褂的恶魔！
t_en: That Dr. Vache wasn't human... he was a devil in a doctor's coat!

t_ch: 我都提醒过她好多次了，这药不能信，可是她……
t_en: I told her so many times not to believe in that medicine, but she...

t_ch: …瓦赫不是已经去世了吗？
t_en: ...I heard that Vache died?

t_ch: …你以为他活着的时候，做的事和「互助会」的那群药贩子有什么区别？要我说，他甚至还不如那帮家伙！
t_en: Yeah... But don't think for a second he was any better than those medicine dealers at the mutual aid society. He was worse than they could ever aspire to be!

t_ch: 「互助会」卖药起码还会告诉买家风险自负，瓦赫那家伙则是免费把那些毒药分发给所有人，连老人和小孩都……
t_en: At least the mutual aid society doesn't hide the risk from the buyers. Vache gave that poison away to everyone for free... even the elderly and the children...

t_ch: 可恶，可恶啊！我妹妹的命怎么就这么苦……
t_en: Argh, I hate them! I hate them all! How could they do this to my sister...

t_ch: 你和她有过交流了吗，{NICKNAME}？
t_en: Did you speak to her, {NICKNAME}?

t_ch: 看你的表情…我想你对那种所谓「特效药」的性质已经有判断了，对吗？
t_en: By the look on your face... I'm assuming you've come to understand the wonder medicine all too well.

t_ch: 推广这种药的人…是恶魔吗？
t_en: The people selling that stuff are devils.

t_ch: 药贩子利用了人们脆弱的一面…
t_en: Those medicine dealers prey on our weaknesses...

t_ch: 嗯，不管是瓦赫还是「互助会」，他们都把下层区人们的心理拿捏得很准。
t_en: Be it Vache or the mutual aid society — forces like that can take full advantage of the... the typical Underworld state of mind.

t_ch: 我给你讲一个故事吧，{NICKNAME}。
t_en: Let me tell you a story, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 曾经，上层区有一位医术高明的医生，他同时还拥有过人的制药天赋。
t_en: There was once a brilliant Overworld doctor with unmatched pharmaceutical skill.

t_ch: 那位医生执意想要研制一种能够使人体不惧严寒的药物，他为自己的研究项目取名「风雪免疫」。
t_en: The doctor was set on developing a medicine that would steel the human body against the cold. He named his research project "Blizzard Immunity."

t_ch: 但医生的研究一再受阻，最大的原因在于缺乏实验对象。篡改人体的免疫系统伴随着巨大的风险，而上层区没有人愿意配合他进行这种实验。
t_en: But his research met with obstruction time and time again, the biggest factor being a lack of test subjects. Tampering with the human immune system carries huge risk, and nobody in the Overworld was willing to submit themselves to such experiments.

t_ch: 于是，那位医生联络了自己在下层区的妹妹…声称他想来支援下层区的医疗工作。
t_en: As such, the doctor got in touch with his sister in the Underworld... claiming that he wanted to support the medical effort below ground.

t_ch: 然后…
t_en: And then...

t_ch: …得知这个消息的妹妹当然喜出望外。毕竟在医学方面，哥哥是她一直以来憧憬的目标。
t_en: ...Naturally, his sister was overjoyed. After all, as far as medicine was concerned, her brother had always been the standard she'd strived to attain.

t_ch: 所以，她亲自把哥哥接到了下层，还托人给他安排了自己的诊所和实验室……
t_en: She brought her brother underground and entrusted him with her clinic and laboratory...

t_ch: …后来的事，你应该能想象得到了。
t_en: ...I think you know what comes next.

t_ch: 抱歉，我之前还怀疑过你…
t_en: I'm sorry that I doubted you before...

t_ch: 那种人居然是娜塔莎的哥哥…
t_en: It's hard to believe he was your brother...

t_ch: 给穷人发放免费的药物，只是瓦赫用来测试实验药剂的手段。
t_en: Issuing free medicine to the needy was just Vache's way of conducting experiments on his test subjects.

t_ch: 他的这些试剂确实能在某种程度上缓解病患的痛苦…但也仅此而已。试剂的效果会让病人暂时忘记痛苦，进而疏漏了常规的治疗……
t_en: On some level, his reagents were able to alleviate the patients' suffering... but nothing more. They would forget their pain for a while, and neglect their regular treatment plans...

t_ch: 结果就是…轻症的患者在虚假的「药效」陪同下痊愈，将一切归功于所谓的「特效药」；而重症患者的病情则会快速加剧，甚至提前迎来死亡。
t_en: The result... patients with mild illness would attribute their recovery to the so-called wonder medicine, while patients with serious illness would quickly deteriorate... and sometimes die.

t_ch: 我不能接受他们把死者的境遇单纯地归结为「不幸」或是「风险」，{NICKNAME}——没有一个称职的医生能接受这种事。
t_en: I cannot accept that their fate was simply the result of misfortune or risk, {NICKNAME}. No true doctor would accept that.

t_ch: 所以，我想正式委托你帮我回收那些「特效药」。你愿意接下这个任务吗？
t_en: That's why I want you to help me recover all the wonder medicine. Are you willing to take on this assignment?

t_ch: 必须有人站出来做正确的事。
t_en: Somebody has to do the right thing.

t_ch: 虽然不是很情愿，但……
t_en: Not my preferred course of action, but...

t_ch: 但你肯定不会坐视不管，对吧？
t_en: ...But it's impossible to sit on the sidelines, right?

t_ch: 谢谢你，{NICKNAME}。
t_en: Thank you, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 这样做一定会让那些已经对「特效药」产生依赖的病人感到痛苦…毕竟要由我们来亲手打碎他们「虚假的希望」。
t_en: This will be hard for the people that have come to rely on the wonder medicine... but it's up to us to remove this false hope.

t_ch: 但还请你不要心软，一定要将瓦赫留下的试剂悉数回收。所有的责任和后果都由我来承担，你不必为此感到焦虑。
t_en: You mustn't hesitate — recover every last reagent that Vache left behind. I'll take responsibility for the consequences — don't worry about that.

t_ch: 去吧，{NICKNAME}。我在这里等着你的好消息。
t_en: Go, {NICKNAME}. I'll be waiting for the good news.

t_ch: 我回来了。
t_en: I'm back.

t_ch: 站住！前面禁止通行！
t_en: Stop right there! No one's allowed up ahead!

t_ch: 把「特效药」交出来吧。
t_en: Hand over the wonder medicine.

t_ch: 嚯！你倒是理直气壮啊，直接明抢是吧？
t_en: Oh-ho! Playing the hero, are we? Is this a stick-up?

t_ch: 你这家伙，就是诊所那个女医生的跟班吧？你以为我们「互助会」没有自己的情报网吗？
t_en: You're that clinic woman's lackey, aren't you. Did you think the mutual aid society didn't have its own intel?

t_ch: 我是来买药的。
t_en: I'm here to buy.

t_ch: 骗谁呢？你这家伙明明就是那个女医生的跟班，你以为我们「互助会」没有自己的情报网吗？
t_en: Who are you trying to kid? You're that clinic woman's lackey. Did you think the mutual aid society didn't have its own intel?

t_ch: 我只是散步经过而已…
t_en: I'm just taking a walk...

t_ch: 到裂界里散步？别装了！
t_en: In the Fragmentum? Gimme a break!

t_ch: 你这家伙，就是诊所那个女医生的跟班吧？你以为我们「互助会」没有自己的情报网吗？
t_en: You're that clinic woman's lackey, aren't you. Did you think the mutual aid society didn't have its own intel?

t_ch: 我看那婆娘就是盯上了我们的药，特地派你来砸场子的吧？
t_en: She's after our medicine and sent you here to smash the place, right?

t_ch: 兄弟们，不用再假装客气了，把这家伙给我赶走！
t_en: Men, look lively and send this idiot packing already!

t_ch: 残破的储物箱里存放着按照实验编号放置的测试药剂，应该就是「互助会」提到的「特效药」了。
t_en: The old box has some medicine labeled with experiment numbers. This must be the wonder medicine the mutual aid society was talking about.

t_ch: 背后突然传来一阵脚步声……
t_en: You suddenly hear footsteps behind you...

t_ch: …好嘛，看看这回我撞上谁了？大名鼎鼎的外来者——没想到还是个小偷兼强盗！
t_en: ...Well, look who we have here. Your reputation precedes you, outsider... I didn't take you for a bandit and a thief!

t_ch: 你是什么人？
t_en: Who are you?

t_ch: 我只是凑巧路过…
t_en: I'm just passing through.

t_ch: 哦，凑巧路过，然后把我们看门的弟兄揍得落花流水？
t_en: Oh, just passing through... And our guards decided to just beat themselves into submission, I suppose?

t_ch: 告诉你吧，我是「疑难重症患者互助会」的会长。你面前这些药物可是我们诸多会员的命根子啊，你确定要做触犯他们利益的事吗？
t_en: You listen here — I'm the president of the Complex Illness Mutual Aid Society. The medicine we hold here is lifeblood for many of our members. Are you sure you want to deprive them of that?

t_ch: 多可怜啊，这些病人…好不容易找到能救命的特效药，居然还要碰上你这种强盗……
t_en: Those poor patients... Along comes a life-saving wonder medicine, only to be snatched away by a shameless bandit like you...

t_ch: 据我所知，这下层区的病患之多，只靠那一家诊所早就容纳不下了吧？
t_en: The Underworld is full of sickness... What would happen if we left it all to that clinic to treat?

t_ch: 有我的「互助会」帮她接手那些已经走投无路的患者，你家那位医生难道不该感谢我吗？
t_en: My mutual aid society has relieved that doctor of patients at the end of the road. Shouldn't she be thanking me?

t_ch: 娜塔莎不会放弃任何患者——
t_en: Natasha would never give up on a patient...

t_ch: ——更不会容忍你这种骗子！
t_en: ...And she'd never work with an impostor like you!

t_ch: 哼，看来你这家伙是铁了心要来砸场子啊……
t_en: Hmm. Seems like you are quite bent on dismantling our operations, aren't you?

t_ch: 也罢，既然被你找到了这里，那说什么也不能让你毫发无损地离开！
t_en: Fine, seeing as you came all this way, it'd be rude not to give you the opportunity!

t_ch: 弟兄们，把这个偷药贼给我拿下！
t_en: Men, seize the medicine thief!

t_ch: 可…可恶…我们和你到底什么仇什么怨，为什么要挡我们的财路……
t_en: Blast it... What did we ever do to you? Why are you taking away our money-maker!?
"...Money-maker"?

t_ch: 看来你也已经承认了啊？自己干这行只是为了赚钱，而不是为了拯救患者的生命。
t_en: So you admit it, then? You're not in this to save lives. You're in it to get rich.

t_ch: 你、你怎么来了？该死，麻烦事一桩接一桩……
t_en: Wh—What are you doing here? Ugh, as if we didn't have enough trouble already...

t_ch: …你早该料到「麻烦」会找上门来，从你决定昧着良心做假药生意的那天开始。
t_en: ...Selling fake medicine with false kindness. You should have expected trouble to find you sooner or later.

t_ch: 我有责任保障患者们的生命安全。换句话说…我必须铲除威胁到他们的危险因素。
t_en: I have a responsibility to protect the well-being of my patients... or to put it another way: I must eradicate any and all threats to them.

t_ch: 你…你救不了所有人！这下层区，茫茫多的病患…你怎么救得过来？
t_en: You... You can't save everyone! The Underworld is teeming with sickness... how could you possibly pull it off!

t_ch: 没错，只靠我自己的力量救不了所有人…如果有必要，我可以放弃吃饭和睡觉的时间来为他们治病。
t_en: You're right, I can't save everyone alone... but if I must, I would sacrifice food and sleep to treat them.

t_ch: 瓦赫欠下层区的债还记在我的头上。我只能尽最大的努力帮助这里的每个人，直到我倒下为止。
t_en: The debt that Vache owes to the Underworld rests with me. All I can do is help everyone to the best of my ability... to the very end.

t_ch: 哼，真是个偏执的家伙…在这方面，你跟你那疯子哥哥没什么两样。
t_en: Ugh... So stubborn... In that sense, you're no different from your crazy brother.

t_ch: …我就当你是在夸奖我了。
t_en: ...I'll take that as a compliment.

t_ch: 谢谢你，{NICKNAME}。你今天的作为能拯救许多下层区的家庭。
t_en: Thank you, {NICKNAME}. Your actions today have saved many Underworld families.

t_ch: 我会找到办法处理哥哥留下的这些试剂，「互助会」再也不能打着他的旗号胡作非为了。
t_en: I'll find a way to dispose of Vache's leftover reagents. Next time the "Mutual Aid Society" wants to whip up a quack cure? They'll be out of luck.

t_ch: 我们回镇上去吧。我还欠那些患者一个解释……
t_en: Let's go back to town. I still owe those patients an explanation...

t_ch: （娜塔莎好像被患者围住了？听听他们在说什么吧…）
t_en: (It looks like the patients have surrounded Natasha? Let's hear what they're saying...)

t_ch: …医生，你说什么？「特效药」已经停止供应了？
t_en: ...What are you saying, doctor? The wonder medicine is out of supply?

t_ch: 对，你没听错。那批药物存在严重的隐患，相关的供应链已经被「地火」切断了。
t_en: Correct. That medicine is a wolf in sheep's clothing — Wildfire has cut off the supply chain.

t_ch: 各位的病情需要合理、科学的治疗，胡乱用药只会加剧你们的症状……
t_en: Your ailments need rational, scientific treatment. Relying on pseudo-medicines will only worsen your condition...

t_ch: 哼，什么合理、科学…我看你就是嫉妒自己的病人被抢走了吧？
t_en: Hmph, rational and scientific, huh...? The way I see it, you're just jealous you lost your patients.

t_ch: 别、别这么跟娜塔莎医生说话，太不礼貌了……
t_en: H—How dare you speak to Dr. Natasha like that! Where are your manners...

t_ch: …不是，老太太，你怎么还向着她啊？我们这特效药吃得好好的，现在说断就断了？凭什么——
t_en: ...Ma'am, please think for a moment. How can you still be siding with her? That wonder medicine was working fine for us, and now it's gone because she wants it to be? How can she—

t_ch: ——我明白你恼火的原因，但还请大家相信我作为主治医生的判断。
t_en: I understand your anger, but as your doctor, I ask you to trust my judgment.

t_ch: 我一定会想办法争取到足够的医疗资源，帮大家渡过各自的难关。如果有必要的话，我会到上层区去求助……
t_en: I will find a way to access more medical resources and help you through your struggles. If I must, I'll get help from the surface...

t_ch: 上层区…哼，我都快忘了，你以前还是个上层区的大小姐呢。
t_en: The surface... Hmph, I almost forgot that you were an Overworld big shot.

t_ch: 你太不尊重娜塔莎医生了……
t_en: Have some respect for Dr. Natasha...

t_ch: 嘁，她最好真能快点找到「特效药」的替代品！否则的话，我就把她陷害瓦赫医生的事告诉所有人。
t_en: Tsk, if it's respect she wants, she better find a replacement for the wonder medicine, fast! If not, I'll tell everyone about how she set up Dr. Vache.

t_ch: ——走了！
t_en: I'm out of here!

t_ch: 啊…你来了，{NICKNAME}。抱歉，被你看到了尴尬的一幕。
t_en: Oh... {NICKNAME}, you're here. I apologize, that must have made for uncomfortable viewing.

t_ch: 你没事吗？
t_en: Are you okay?

t_ch: 嗯…我没事。设身处地地想想，他会有那样的反应也很自然。我能做的只有尽力为这些患者治疗，消除他们的疑虑。
t_en: Mm... I'm okay. If I were in his shoes, I may well have had the same reaction. Now I need to focus on treating the patients, and dispelling their doubts.

t_ch: 好想修理那个嘴碎的家伙！
t_en: I'd like to straighten out that loudmouth myself!

t_ch: 别冲动。设身处地地想想，他会有那样的反应也很自然。我能做的只有尽力为这些患者治疗，消除他们的疑虑。
t_en: Don't let your emotions get the better of you. If I were in his shoes, I might well have had the same reaction. Now I need to focus on treating the patients, and dispelling their doubts.

t_ch: …先不提这个了。收拾掉了瓦赫留下的烂摊子，我心里那块石头也能暂时放下。
t_en: ...Let's leave it for now. I'll feel a lot better once I've gotten rid of this mess that Vache left us.

t_ch: 现在…我想跟你完整地讲讲有关他的事。
t_en: ...Now's as good a time as any to tell you Vache's whole story.

t_ch: 你如果有任何想问我的事，就请现在开口吧。
t_en: If there's anything you want to know, don't hesitate to ask.

t_ch: 他是你的亲生哥哥吗？
t_en: Was he your biological brother?

t_ch: 不…但我一直以为，我们的关系比同父同母的兄妹更亲密。
t_en: No... but I used to think we were so close that we may as well have been...

t_ch: 我其实也是在孤儿院长大的孩子。直到被来自上层区的一家人收养以后，我的人生轨迹才发生了变化。
t_en: I actually grew up in the orphanage. It wasn't until I was adopted by an Overworld family that life changed for me.

t_ch: 我的养母，她一直将我视若己出。瓦赫也把我当亲妹妹一样疼爱…至少儿时如此。
t_en: My adoptive mother always saw me as her own flesh and blood, and Vache loved me like a sister... that is, while we were still young.

t_ch: 养父母一家是医学世家，我们俩从小就接触到了很多医学专著。长大以后，我们也都顺利进入了贝洛伯格的医学院校，走上了从医这条路。
t_en: My adoptive parents belonged to a prominent medical family — as kids, we were exposed to all kinds of medical texts. When we were older, both of us got straight into Belobog's medical school. We were on a path into the medical profession.

t_ch: 瓦赫是个什么样的人？
t_en: What kind of person was Vache?

t_ch: 温和，内向。但是一旦涉及到专业领域…他就会变得极端偏执，像是换了一副脸孔。
t_en: He was gentle and introverted. But when it came to work he could be absurdly obsessive... like a completely different person.

t_ch: 如果我在实验时犯了错误，他会用尖刻的词句苛责我…但事后又会道歉，安慰我。他就是如此矛盾的一个人。
t_en: Whenever I made a mistake in an experiment, he'd bite my head off... and then a while later, he'd apologize and comfort me. He was conflicted in that way.

t_ch: 你早就知道这药是他留下的？
t_en: So you knew this medicine was his work?

t_ch: 我并不知道他还在铆钉镇留下了残余的试剂。刚听你说起时，我也有些震惊。
t_en: I didn't know he had leftover reagents in Rivet Town. I was shocked when you told me.

t_ch: 没能彻底斩断联系的过往…总有一天会回来敲你的门。
t_en: If you fail to make a clean break with the past, the past creeps up on you...

t_ch: 他…真的已经不在了？
t_en: Did he...really pass away?

t_ch: 这件事得从头说起…瓦赫从小就有一个理想——研制出名为「风雪免疫」的药物。
t_en: *sigh* That's a long story... Ever since he was little, Vache had always wanted to develop a medicine called "Blizzard Immunity."

t_ch: 他将人体的畏寒性视作疾病，希望能研制出与之对抗的药物，让人类能在寒潮中生存。那样的话，人们就可以走出贝洛伯格，在雪原中重建家园。
t_en: He saw the human body's vulnerability to cold as a disease, and wanted to cure it so that humanity could survive the Eternal Freeze. If he succeeded, people would be able to leave Belobog and rebuild their homes in the Snow Plains.

t_ch: 你相信他的理想吗？
t_en: Do you share his vision?

t_ch: 老实说，我不知道「风雪免疫」该如何达成。但我敬佩他的理想和坚持…所以从小到大，我一直选择相信他。
t_en: To be honest, I never understood how he'd achieve that "Blizzard Immunity." But I do admire that vision and perseverance... I always chose to believe him, ever since I was a kid.

t_ch: （适当的沉默）
t_en: (Brief silence)

t_ch: 我敬佩他的理想和坚持，虽然我觉得「风雪免疫」是个遥不可及的理想。
t_en: I do admire his vision and perseverance, even though "Blizzard Immunity" always seemed unachievable to me.

t_ch: 我知道他在上层的研究并不顺利，所以经常给他写信，让他到下层来看看，和孤儿院的孩子们待上一段时间——权当是一种调剂。
t_en: I knew his research in the Overworld wasn't going well. I used to write to him and ask him to come down and stay with the kids in the orphanage for a while. I thought it might help take his mind off his research.

t_ch: 但当时我还太年轻，还不知道长期的挫败会如何压垮一个人，给他带去可怕的影响……
t_en: But I was too young to know what long-term frustration could do to a person. It changed him in a terrible way...

t_ch: 「可怕的影响」…？
t_en: Changed him...?

t_ch: 在下层的那段时间，他接触到了一些受到轻微裂界侵蚀影响的患者。他们的身体对冷热的反应异于常人，这种特性让瓦赫十分着迷。
t_en: During his stay in the Underworld, he met patients affected by Fragmentum corrosion with mild symptoms. Compared with non-patients, their bodies reacted differently to hot and cold. And that fascinated Vache.

t_ch: 他开始尝试复现轻微的裂界侵蚀效果，认为这种研究能促进「风雪免疫」的开发……
t_en: He began to simulate mild Fragmentum corrosion, thinking that it could lead to breakthroughs in developing Blizzard Immunity.

t_ch: 这些试剂并不能治愈一切重疾——世界上不存在那种万能药。瓦赫制作出它们，只是为了在短期内稳定受研究者的症状…以便观察他们的体征变化。
t_en: Those reagents of his aren't cure-alls for critical illness — that doesn't exist. Vache made the reagents to mitigate the suffering of his patients in the short term — a convenient way for him to observe changes in his test subjects.

t_ch: 在我没有注意到的角落，他一直在向无辜的居民分发着这种试剂。等我注意到异常时…他的理智已经快被可怕的研究消磨殆尽了。
t_en: But behind my back, he had been doling out the reagents to residents who were kept in the dark. When it finally struck me that something was wrong, his terrible research had already turned him into... into a madman.

t_ch: 他已经丧失人性了…
t_en: He'd lost his last shred of humanity...

t_ch: 嗯…而且这一切就发生在我的眼皮底下。想来可笑，对亲人的信任竟然蒙蔽了我的双眼……
t_en: Indeed... and it all happened under my nose. How ridiculous — my judgment was clouded by trust in a loved one...

t_ch: 过了很久，你什么都没发现？
t_en: Did nothing seem strange to you all that time?

t_ch: 当时的我无条件地信任瓦赫。想来真是可笑又可悲……
t_en: I trusted Vache with all my heart. Funny, isn't it? And pathetic.

t_ch: 他的实验被揭发以后，我和奥列格商议了很久，讨论该如何给这件事善后。
t_en: When his experiments were exposed, I spent a long time with Oleg discussing how to handle the aftermath.

t_ch: 当时正值封锁令颁布前夕，下层的银鬃铁卫已经近乎不见踪影，我们想了很久都找不到处置瓦赫的办法。
t_en: This was shortly before the Underworld was sealed off — there were hardly any Silvermane Guards remaining. We spent ages trying to find a way to handle Vache.

t_ch: 最后，奥列格建议将他驱逐了事，并且永远禁止他再踏足下层。
t_en: In the end, Oleg suggested that we banish him and permanently deny him access to the Underworld.

t_ch: 如果让他的受害者知道了真相，瓦赫肯定会被愤怒的下层区人撕成碎片。那时的我还很年轻、软弱，奥列格会提出那种建议，肯定也是顾及了我的感情。
t_en: Had his victims found out the truth, Vache would've been torn to pieces by furious Underworlders. I was young and weak at the time — Oleg must have worried about how I'd react to such a scenario, or he wouldn't have suggested banishment.

t_ch: 这处罚未免太轻了…
t_en: Banishment isn't a particularly harsh punishment...

t_ch: 现在回想起来，的确如此。他的所作所为值得更加严苛的惩罚……
t_en: *sigh* Looking back, it wasn't. He deserved much worse...

t_ch: 你们做了正确的事。
t_en: You two did the right thing.

t_ch: …希望如此。得知真相的那天…我第一次觉得兄长的面貌如此陌生。
t_en: ...I hope so. The day I learned the truth was the day I no longer recognized my brother.

t_ch: 我和奥列格把他带回了上层。瓦赫最后向我们提出的要求，是将他带到城市之外，他一直想要战胜的那片雪原之中。
t_en: Oleg and I took Vache to the Overworld. He made his final request to us: To take him outside the city to the Snow Plains he'd always wanted to conquer.

t_ch: 就在我的眼前，他独自走进了雪幕…从那时候起，我就不断在脑海里对自己重复一句话——「瓦赫已经死了」。
t_en: His figure gradually disappeared into the snow curtain... I've been telling myself that Vache is dead ever since.

t_ch: 抱歉，{NICKNAME}，我不太擅长讲故事。瓦赫是位糟糕透顶的兄长，更是个不称职的医生…你只要知道这点就够了。
t_en: Forgive me, {NICKNAME}, I'm not good at telling stories. Vache was a terrible brother and a worse doctor... That's all you need to know.

t_ch: 今天就聊到这里吧，谢谢你帮我处理哥哥留下的烂摊子。这是一点谢意，请你收下。
t_en: Let's call it a day. Thank you for cleaning up the mess my brother left behind. Here — for your trouble.

t_ch: 目前，您能参加的是我们新赛季的揭幕战。规则很简单，您将连续挑战五名来自下层区各地的传奇拳手，在战斗中取胜即可获得豪华奖励！
t_en: Currently, you can participate in our new season opener match. The rules are simple, you will challenge five legendary Underworld boxers in a row, and win the battle for a deluxe prize!

t_ch: 揭幕战过后，下一赛季的比赛便会正式打响——相关事项目前仍在持续紧密筹备中，敬请期待！
t_en: After the season opener, the season's super league will officially begin! Related matters are still undergoing continuous, intensive preparation, so stay tuned!

t_ch: 哟，您来了！揭幕战下一场对手已经就绪，观众们也都迫不及待了——如果您也准备停当，我们即刻为您安排开赛！
t_en: Yo, you're here! Your next opponent for the tournament is ready, and the crowd couldn't wait any longer. The match will start as soon as you got ready, so chop-chop!

t_ch: 下一场比赛即将开始——{NICKNAME}，你做好准备了吗？
t_en: The next match is starting soon. Are you ready, {NICKNAME}?

t_ch: 让我们继续吧！
t_en: Let us continue!

t_en: Contestant {NICKNAME} is ready to continue the match! Who will be {M#his}{F#her} next opponent? Put your hands together for...

t_en: I need to take a rest...

t_ch: 没问题，这座八角笼与全场观众时刻等候你的归来！
t_en: No problem, the octagon and the audience await your return!

t_ch: 接下来的一段时间，搏击俱乐部会为新赛季做充分准备。相关前期工作目前已在紧密筹备中，敬请期待！
t_en: Now the Fight Club will be preparing for the new season. Planning and preparation for the new season are already underway, please stay tuned!

t_ch: 正式开赛时，搏击俱乐部将会向您告知——届时若想了解详情，掘掘博士随时为您服务！

t_en: After the opening matches, you can now join the leagues and accumulate points in the current season!

t_en: The all-new season of Fight Club has begun!\nFor further information, go ahead and ask Dr. Dig about it at the Fight Club itself!

t_en: Fight Club's all-new season has begun — You can now register for the featherweight matches!

t_ch: 若您想要了解详情，或是参加比赛，都请随时告诉我！掘掘博士竭诚为您服务！
t_en: If you want more information or to join the matches, feel free to ask me about it! I will do my best to give you all you need!

t_ch: 积分赛的具体规则是什么？
t_en: What are the rules for the point-based tournament?

t_ch: 您听说过「天梯排行榜」吗？也就是记录了每位拳手比赛积分的榜单。您的积分越高，在榜上排的位置也就越高。只要您在对战中取胜，就能获得一定积分。
t_en: Have you heard of the Ladder Leaderboard? It's a list recording every boxer's match points. The higher your points, the higher the rank. You will earn points for every battle you win.

t_ch: 换句话说，分数越高的拳手实力越强，您和对手皆是如此。这也意味着随着您胜利的次数增加，战斗强度也会随之增大——不过，这点我猜您早有准备，对吧？
t_en: In other words, the higher the points, the stronger the boxer, for both you and your opponent. It also means that the more you win, the harder it gets. But I guess you're ready for that, right?

t_ch: 此外，我们还为不同实力的选手分别设置了不同的赛事，难度由低到高依次为——
t_en: Side note, we also prepared different leagues for participants of different strengths, and they are, in order...

t_ch: 「羽量级」、「轻量级」、「沉量级」、「中量级」、「重量级」——每个排行榜的积分各自独立计算！
t_en: Featherweight, lightweight, welterweight, middleweight, and heavyweight... And the score on each board is calculated individually!

t_ch: 您可以任意选择其一，与不同水平的选手对垒。只要您愿意，更可以将自己在全部赛事中的排名全部提升至第一，从而将赛季全部奖励收入囊中！
t_en: You can choose one of them and face off with opponents of different levels. You can even place first in all of the matches and take all the rewards of the season by yourself!

t_ch: 不过，五大赛事将依次隔天开启——若您想要体验更加激烈的战斗，可千万别扑空了！
t_en: However, each of the five leagues will start on the following day in turn. If you want to experience a heart-racing battle, don't miss out!

t_en: I want to join!

t_en: Dear Lord, you've conquered all five of the leagues! Outsiders are such frightening forces of nature!

t_ch: 随着{NICKNAME}您在本赛季中一路高歌猛进、彻底扫清一切障碍，本赛季也即将迎来尾声——
t_en: {NICKNAME}, as you charge your way through the current season's matches and clear all obstacles from your path to greatness, the super league has come to a close...

t_en: I hereby announce: The season has come to a successful conclusion, congratulations on becoming the champion! Scott Business Alliance has prepared a large number of luxurious rewards for you, please be sure to collect them!

t_ch: <size=36>{NICKNAME}！{NICKNAME}！{NICKNAME}！</size>
t_en: <size=36>{NICKNAME}! {NICKNAME}! {NICKNAME}!</size>

t_ch: 传说中的外来者一路披荆斩棘，揭幕战全胜宝座已是近在眼前！但{M#他}{F#她}能够像往常一样轻易击败擂主吗？让我们拭目以待！
t_en: The legendary outsider has climbed {M#his}{F#her} way to the very top, and the season opener win is so close at hand, but will {NICKNAME} beat the champ as easily as usual? Let us wait and see!

t_ch: <size=36>「灰背鲸」——以及它完全升级的遥控支援系统「龙骑兵」！</size>
t_en: <size=36>Grayback Whale, and its fully upgraded remote support system, the Dragon Rider!</size>

t_ch: 听听，这名字多唬人啊！这场对决，将会决定赛季揭幕战的终极大奖花落谁家——
t_en: Listen here, what a name it is! This battle will decide the one who lays claim to the season opener ultimate prize!

t_ch: 榜首宝座是否会在这最后关头再度易手，将成为本战的最大看点！
t_en: Will the championship change hands at this very last moment? This will be the highlight of the match!

t_ch: 让我们将热烈的掌声献给两位斗士吧！现在，我宣布——
t_en: Let's give it up for the two warriors — Now, I'm proud to announce that...

t_ch: <size=36>巅峰争霸战，正式开始！</size>
t_en: <size=36>The battle for the top begins!</size>

t_ch: <size=36>{NICKNAME}！{NICKNAME}！{NICKNAME}！</size>
t_en: <size=36>{NICKNAME}! {NICKNAME}! {NICKNAME}!</size>

t_ch: <size=36>{NICKNAME}！{NICKNAME}！{NICKNAME}！</size>
t_en: <size=36>{NICKNAME}! {NICKNAME}! {NICKNAME}!</size>

t_ch: 我现在就想参加下一场比赛。
t_en: I want to be in the next match now.

t_ch: 恭喜您，在本赛季揭幕战中五战全胜！您自然应得到丰厚的奖励——史考特老板早就安排好了，请您一定拿好！
t_en: Congratulations on your season opener win in all five of the matches! And of course, Mr. Scott has arranged for your rich reward. Here you go!

t_ch: 但如果您还想和我们的拳手过过招，也请告诉我！掘掘博士立刻为您安排！
t_en: But if you still wanted to spar with our boxers, lemme know! I can arrange it for you immediately!

t_ch: 没事了。
t_en: That's all.

t_ch: 若您有想法了，随时与我联系即可！
t_en: No worries, if a question does arise, feel free to ask me anytime!

t_ch: 您稍等片刻——我这就去通知各部门，准备开始下一场比赛！
t_en: Just a moment, I'm going to inform all departments that the next fight is about to begin!

t_ch: 「军团」远去，奇物归位，各科室研究再度开张，新进宇宙舶来品快速流转……
t_en: The Legion is gone, and the Curios are back. Various departments are returning to their research, and the newly imported goods from the cosmos are beginning to circulate.

t_ch: 「复兴计划」诸事顺利，那么这封信，究竟在指什么？
t_en: Everything is going smoothly with Project Revival. So what exactly does this letter mean?

t_ch: 听说你找我？
t_en: I heard you are looking for me?

t_ch: 是{NICKNAME}回来啦，你来得正巧！
t_en: {NICKNAME}, you're back. Just in time!

t_ch: 在全体科员和你这位得力帮手的共同建设之下，「黑塔」已经大致恢复了战前的元气。
t_en: Thanks to your help and everybody's hard work, the space station is pretty much back on its feet.

t_ch: 在复兴的尾声，我本来是想把你叫回来做个见证，也算有始有终。
t_en: As a conclusion to Project Revival, I wanted to summon you to witness the fruits of our labor.

t_ch: 但就在前两天，我收到了一封奇怪的信，既然你来了，也帮我拿拿主意。
t_en: But just two days ago, I received a strange letter. Now that you're here, perhaps you can help me figure out what it is about.

t_ch: 我常听科员称赞你的多谋和善断，怎么样，对这段文字有什么头绪吗？
t_en: I often hear the researchers praise you for your resourcefulness and good judgment. What do you think? Any clue what this message is about?

t_ch: 很像应物科的风格。
t_en: It seems like something from the Department of Implement Arts.

t_ch: 没错，只有应物科的温姓科员会这么说话。
t_en: Yes, only the researcher called Wen in the Department of Implement Arts would speak like that.

t_ch: 然而此信来源蹊跷。它被夹在议事间桌上那本名叫《星域界种指南》的大部头中间，平时除我之外，没有其他人翻看。
t_en: However, it was a strange story how I got hold of this letter at the beginning. It was sandwiched in a tome called A Guide to Astral Realm Species on the Conference Room table. Normally, no one looks at it except me.

t_ch: 显然，有人想把这封信私下递给我，所以我也不敢贸然询问应物科的人。
t_en: Obviously, someone wanted to hand me this letter in private, so I didn't dare to ask the staff in the Department of Implement Arts.

t_ch: 宓堂空境解语人是谁？
t_en: Who is this Cryptologist From Locus Silentii?

t_ch: 据我所知，空间站里没有顶着这个名号的科员，应该只是化名。
t_en: As far as I know, there is no one with this title in the space station. It must be an alias.

t_ch: 但写信人的身份，并不是我的关注重点啦。
t_en: But the identity of the writer is not what I am most concerned about.

t_ch: 魈魅听着尤其耳熟。
t_en: Spirit sounds particularly familiar.

t_ch: 魈魅…魈魅…不会是…水魈？不愧是{NICKNAME}呀，一下就抓住了全文的「文眼」。现在一切都说得通了！
t_en: Spirit... Spirit... Can it be... The Xenohydro? Well done, {NICKNAME}! You just found the "key" to the text right away. Now everything makes sense!

t_ch: 看来，这并不是普通的信件，而是一封「告密信」，意在检举「异星水魈」。
t_en: It seems this is no ordinary letter, it's from an informant. Their intention is to report on the Xenohydro.

t_ch: 自从「异星水魈」从温明德队长手下脱逃之后，它就从空间站销声匿迹。
t_en: The Xenohydro has disappeared from the space station since it escaped from Mr. Wen Mingde's hands.

t_ch: 这封信，似乎印证了曾有的传言——水魈迟迟没有落网，正是因为有共犯包庇。
t_en: This letter seems to support the rumor that the Xenohydro was not caught because it has an accomplice.

t_ch: 你看这下阙，「细问魈魅踪迹，偏看收容一隅」明显是在指引我们去查探水魈的活动范围；而「戚戚与之同谋」，则点明了共犯的存在。
t_en: Look at this sentence. "An inquiry into spirit traces, a glance into the site of storage" is obviously leading us to investigate the Xenohydro's range of activities, and "to be an accomplice" implies the existence of an accomplice.

t_ch: 至于其他六言句，依我来看，一部分描绘了这位共犯的画像，一部分表述了水魈真正的藏匿地点。可惜，我对如何破解剩下的信息毫无头绪。
t_en: As for the other six sentences, in my opinion, some depict the portrait of the accomplice and some describe the real hiding place of the Xenohydro. Unfortunately, I have no idea how to interpret the rest of the message.

t_ch: 如今工作告一段落，恰逢这封告密信出现，是时候借着东风，把水魈议题重新摆上台面。{NICKNAME}，你怎么看？
t_en: Now that we are done with other work, this letter falls into our hands at just the right moment. It's high time we put the Xenohydro issue back on the table. {NICKNAME}, what do you think?

t_ch: 有始有终，我赞成！
t_en: I am with you all the way!

t_ch: 最近身上有不少事…
t_en: I have a lot going on lately...

t_ch: 希望{NICKNAME}你不要推辞。毕竟——
t_en: Please, {NICKNAME}. I hope you won't refuse. After all—

t_ch: 考虑到有内鬼卷入此事，如果交由普通科员来办，难免打草惊蛇。而{NICKNAME}既非科员，又和水魈打过照面，可以说是「唯一」的理想人选。
t_en: Considering there may be a traitor among us, if I hand this case to our staff, we might alert the culprit. And {NICKNAME}, you're not a staff member, and you've also met the Xenohydro before. You really are the ideal candidate to handle this case.

t_ch: 一旦牵扯到共犯，这件事就不再是单纯的奇物收容问题，必须谨慎处理。所以，我想请你先到信中明确提到的收容舱段，探探这段言论的虚实。
t_en: And if there is an accomplice involved, this matter is no longer a simple case of Curio retrieval. It must be handled with caution. Therefore, I would like to ask you to go to the Storage Zone mentioned in the letter and verify the facts.

t_ch: 只要{NICKNAME}能抓住水魈或是共犯中的任何一个，我们就能当面对质。至于那位科员是一念之差，还是有心作恶，我会作出公正评判。
t_en: If we can catch either the Xenohydro or the accomplice, we'll be able to interrogate them. Then I can judge whether the accomplice's motive is malicious or not.

t_ch: 在这大势所趋的关头，就让复兴计划在你我手中画上句点吧。
t_en: At this critical juncture, it is up to us to finally finish Project Revival.

t_ch: ——「波少」一向大人有大量，其实，你完全有机会重获我的青睐…只要你肯为了之前的那些行为道歉！
t_en: I've always been a rather magnanimous person. In fact, here is a chance for you to regain my favor... As long as you are willing to apologize for your behavior!

t_ch: 那些事又不是我干的……
t_en: I didn't do those things...

t_ch: 想否认事实？！拉明娜啊拉明娜，你的花样可是越来越多了！我来帮你回忆回忆——
t_en: You want to deny the truth!? Lamina, Lamina, you're getting more and more dishonest! Let me help you remember—

t_ch: 我盒饭中的芥末、制服背后的大洞、回复女科员的干瘪信件，这些难道不是你的手笔？
t_en: The mustard in my lunch box, the big hole on the back of my uniform, and this boring letter in reply to the female researcher. Aren't they your doing?

t_ch: 虽然那些东西确实只有我经手过，但……
t_en: It's true I'm the only person who handled those things, but...

t_ch: 更别说，当我在跟艾丝妲小姐汇报工作的时候，你还突然大叫，卡波特先生，来不及了，「呜呜伯」还在等着您和它们一起玩呢！让小姐以为我私通奇物，不务正业！
t_en: And when I was reporting to Miss Asta, you suddenly shouted, "Mr. Capote, it's too late. The Wubbaboos are still waiting for you to play with them!" You made her think I was colluding with Curios and not doing my job.

t_ch: 可这句话的确是在陈述事实啊……
t_en: But I was just stating a fact...

t_ch: 天知地知，你知我知！这句话我跟你说了多少遍，你就是存心要和我作对！
t_en: We should keep it only between you, and me! How many times have I told you that? You were just going against me on purpose!

t_ch: 算了，我看你根本没有悔改的意思，等你哪天在这里呆得头晕眼花，前途灰暗，你自然会再回来寻求「波少」的帮助。
t_en: Forget it. I don't think you are going to repent at all. One day, when you're sick of this place and your future is gloomy, you will come crawling back to seek help from me.

t_ch: 你就祈祷那时候我还没找到什么薇诺娜埃琳娜芭芭娜之类的科员来顶替你吧！哼。
t_en: Just keep praying that by then, I haven't replaced you with a Renona, an Elena, or a Barbara... or someone else, hmph!

t_ch: …我到底什么时候才能变得更勇敢一点……
t_en: When can I become braver?

t_ch: 啊，是空间站的英雄{NICKNAME}，久闻大名，没想到有机会近距离见到……
t_en: Oh, it's {NICKNAME}, the hero of the space station. I've heard so much about you. I didn't expect to have the opportunity to see you up close.

t_ch: 你在这里干什么？
t_en: What are you doing here?

t_ch: 我…我本来是卡波特先生的秘书。
t_en: I... I was Mr. Capote's secretary.

t_ch: 最近帮他办事，出了些岔子，就被他赶到这里，做奇物分拣的流水线工作。
t_en: But recently, I made a few mistakes when I was running errands for him. So he sent me here to work on the Curio sorting assembly line.

t_ch: 真是不好意思，让你看笑话啦……
t_en: I'm so sorry, you must think I am a joke...

t_ch: 那些事是你干的？
t_en: You did all those things?

t_ch: 都被你听到啦…其实卡波特先生安在我头上的那些罪名，我根本没有印象。
t_en: You heard it all... Although, I don't remember doing the things Mr. Capote accused me of.

t_ch: 至于真正的罪犯，我也怀疑过另有其人，但无论是监控还是其他证据显示，卡波特先生的东西确实只有我一个人碰过。
t_en: I also suspected someone else, but everything showed that I was the only one who touched Mr. Capote's things.

t_ch: 也许真应了那句界种科内部的「拉明娜定律」，凡是我拉明娜「有可能会」搞砸的事，「就一定会」被拉明娜搞砸……
t_en: Maybe "Lamina's law" is really true. In the Department of Ecology, they have a saying, anything that Lamina "can" screw up, Lamina will "screw up."

t_ch: 先别急着下判断。
t_en: Don't rush to judgment yet.

t_ch: 难道{NICKNAME}觉得…还有其他的可能？不可能吧……
t_en: {NICKNAME}, do you think... there's another explanation for this? Surely not...

t_ch: 很有意思的定律！
t_en: That's an interesting law!

t_ch: 别这样，要是它叫做「{NICKNAME}定律」，你会怎么想……
t_en: Come on, how would you feel if it was called the "{NICKNAME}'s law."

t_ch: 有封信想请你看看。
t_en: I have a letter for you to read.

t_ch: 以我的能力，估计帮不上什么忙…我先看看吧…
t_en: Sure, but knowing me, I probably won't be much help...

t_ch: 嗯，如果我没理解错的话，这封信里，「细问魈魅踪迹」似乎写的是我们界种科的奇物「异星水魈」。而「戚戚与之同谋」，则像是某种指控……
t_en: Well, if I understand correctly, where it says, "An inquiry into spirit traces," it seems to be referring to the Xenohydro, a kind of Curio from our department. And "to be an accomplice" seems to be some kind of accusation.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}为什么问我这个，莫非，你正在调查「异星水魈」窜逃案吗？
t_en: {NICKNAME}, why are you asking me this? Are you investigating the escape of the Xenohydro?

t_ch: 它就在收容舱段。
t_en: It's in the storage zone.

t_ch: 啊？！是吗…我最近一直呆在这边，倒是没有发现异常。
t_en: Oh!? Really... I've been hanging around here lately, but I haven't noticed anything unusual.

t_ch: 它也许冒充了你？
t_en: Maybe it's impersonating you?

t_ch: 啊？！难道卡波特先生说的那些事，也是它冒充我做的？可它没必要这样做吧……
t_en: Huh!? Did it do those things that Mr. Capote is accusing me of? But why...

t_ch: …至于信上的其他内容，恕我愚笨，我实在是看不大懂。
t_en: As for the rest of the letter, please forgive my ignorance, but I really don't understand it.

t_ch: 但既然有了收容舱段这个线索，这里的区域拢共就这么大，不如优先采取枚举法或者排除法。
t_en: But now we know it's in the Storage Zone, and this area is only so large. Maybe we can take the initiative and make a list of the possible suspects.

t_ch: 这块地方我还算熟，记忆中的确有一两处地方人迹罕至，有可能窝藏水魈，我可以带你去碰碰运气。
t_en: I'm quite familiar with this place. There are one or two places I know that are inaccessible where the Xenohydro may be hiding. I can take you to have a look.

t_ch: 「行动」总比「空想」要好，如果不嫌弃的话，{NICKNAME}你就跟上来吧。
t_en: Actions are better than words. If you don't mind, {NICKNAME}, follow me.

t_ch: 咳、咳，这房间，好浓重的水腥气…看来水魈真的来过这里。
t_en: Ahem, ehem, this room smells humid... It seems the Xenohydro really was here.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}你快过来，这里据点隐蔽，视野开阔，可以监控整个房间。
t_en: {NICKNAME}, come quickly. This place is secluded and it's a good vantage point. You can watch the whole room from here.

t_ch: 那个…我们确定要在这里蹲它吗？其实可以试着做个捕猎装置，也算是我们界种科的工作规范。
t_en: Well... Are you sure we want to ambush it here? We can try making a trap, it's one of our standard procedures in the Department of Ecology.

t_ch: 装置要怎么做？
t_en: How do you make a trap?

t_ch: 直接人肉蹲它。
t_en: Ambush it in person.

t_ch: 这样也许不太明智…按照《星域界种指南》的说法，水魈这种生物行踪诡秘，又生性多疑，简单的守株待兔，大概率会竹篮打水一场空。
t_en: This may not be wise... According to the A Guide to Species in the Astral Realm, the Xenohydro is very cautious and stealthy. If we simply wait for it, it's probably just a waste of time.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，你不如先听听捕猎装置这个方案……
t_en: {NICKNAME}, why don't I show you how to make a trap?

t_ch: 「将两只一次性动力臂严丝合缝地铰接，一旦猎物以微弱的生物震动踏入动力臂的捕捉范围，等待它们的便是在劫难逃的命运。」
t_en: Tightly connect the two disposable kinetic arms. Once the prey steps within range of the power arm, all that awaits them is doom.

t_ch: 而要做一次性动力臂，我们需要找到许多精细的零件、毁灭异质、相位灵火……
t_en: To make a disposable kinetic arm, we need to find many intricate parts, Strange Matter of Destruction and Phase Flame...

t_ch: 得亏你记性好才能记住。
t_en: I am glad you have such a good memory.

t_ch: 不不，「星域界种」就是这样一门非常严谨、优雅的学科，我只是如实传达出它的魅力。
t_en: No, astral ecology is a rigorous and beautiful discipline of knowledge. I am simply conveying its charm truthfully.

t_ch: 但你提醒了我，似乎一时间很难把这么多材料凑齐……
t_en: But you reminded me that it'll be difficult to gather all the materials quickly.

t_ch: 听起来是很高级的材料。
t_en: Sounds like fancy material.

t_ch: 也还好啦，以前我们可是想向应物科支取多少就能支取多少。不过，现在物资不比以前宽裕，短时间内很难凑齐……
t_en: It's okay. In the past, we could take as much as we wanted from the Department of Implement Arts. But now we are short of materials, it'll be hard to collect everything we need soon.

t_ch: 做完水魈已经老死了吧。
t_en: By that time the Xenohydro will have died of old age.

t_ch: 啊，用不了那么久吧？水魈的寿命应该还挺长的…不过把精力聚焦在装置上，确实容易陷入被动，本末倒置。
t_en: Well, it won't take quite that long. The Xenohydro has a pretty long lifespan... But we probably shouldn't put ourselves in such a passive situation.

t_ch: 抱歉跟你说了这么多，我们还是靠人肉蹲点吧……
t_en: Anyway, we'd better ambush it ourselves.

t_ch: 或许要等上一段时间，不如聊聊天打发打发时间吧，{NICKNAME}。
t_en: We may have to wait for a while. Why don't we have a chat to kill time, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 为什么帮卡波特办事？
t_en: Why do you work for Capote?

t_ch: 你不是第一个问这问题的人…撇开「波少」的粉丝不谈，很多人对卡波特先生印象糟糕，认为他行事张扬，仗势欺人。
t_en: You're not the first person to ask me this question... Except for his fans, many people have a bad impression of Master Capote. They think he is loud and obnoxious.

t_ch: 但我和他相处的时间最长，我知道，卡波特先生本性不坏，他只是拥有很多优越的条件，因此没办法理解别人的困境。简单来说，他只是被运气「宠坏」了。
t_en: But I have been with him for the longest time. I know that Mr. Capote is not a bad person in nature. He is just naturally gifted in many aspects, so he can't understand other people's difficulties sometimes. In short, he is just spoiled by fate.

t_ch: 怎么被运气「宠坏」？
t_en: How can you be spoiled by fate?

t_ch: 得天独厚的帅气长相、老天爷赏饭吃的研究才华、轻易被人喜欢的飒爽风度…一个人从来都这样如意，要求所有人都围着自己转，也是很自然的事吧。
t_en: With his unusually handsome appearance, his great talent for research, and his lovable outgoing nature... Everything has always worked out well for him. So it's normal for him to think the world revolves around him.

t_ch: 你还在帮他说话。
t_en: You're still defending him.

t_ch: 没、没有，我只是陈述我眼睛所看到的东西而已。从小，家里人就让我多关注事情的亮面，这样生活才不会那么阴郁。
t_en: No, no, I'm just stating what I see. Since my childhood, my family has always taught me to focus on the positive, so that life wouldn't be so gloomy.

t_ch: 更何况，他确实给了我一份能更接近界种科本真研究的工作机会，虽然代价是要兼任他的秘书……
t_en: Not to mention he did give me a job that lets me get closer to the real research work in the Department of Ecology. Although the price was to work as his secretary...

t_ch: 拉明娜怎么看自己的？
t_en: How do you see yourself, Lamina?

t_ch: 我、我么…就是一个再普通不过的小科员，甚至这么说都是高看了我自己……
t_en: Me? I... I'm just an ordinary researcher. Even that sounds like an exaggeration.

t_ch: 因为…我是顶替了姐姐的名额，才得以进入「黑塔」…当然这件事，卡波特先生也不知道。
t_en: Because... I only got into the space station in my sister's place... Of course, Mr. Capote doesn't know about that.

t_ch: 我的母星资源贫乏，每个家庭只能勉强支撑一个人进行星际旅行。姐姐获得了「黑塔」的遴选资格，但她把这全家独一、毕生唯一的机会，让给了我。
t_en: My home planet is quite poor in resources, and most families can only barely support one person to go on a space journey. My sister was qualified to come to the space station, but she gave me the only chance in the family and in her life.

t_ch: 可惜我不争气，如果是姐姐在黑塔，一定能干出一番名头。所以我是怎样的人？这个问题，我无法…或是说，根本不敢回答……
t_en: It's a pity I am such a failure. If my sister were here in the space station, she would be able to make a name for herself. So how do I see myself? I can't... Or dare not answer this question at all...

t_ch: 没什么想聊的了。
t_en: That's all for now.

t_ch: 你和拉明娜在这里蹲到辰宿列张，露结为霜，肚皮空荡荡。
t_en: You and Lamina wait there until very late. The dew has turned to frost, and your belly is empty.

t_ch: 但是什么也没有发生。
t_en: But nothing happened.

t_ch: …这样等下去似乎不是办法……
t_en: We can't just wait here forever...

t_ch: 呼…水魈还是影子也没有…也许，它早就离开了？
t_en: *sigh* No sign of the Xenohydro... Maybe it left a long time ago?

t_ch: 我们可以找找房间里的使用痕迹，通过新旧程度，大致能够推算水魈离开的时间。
t_en: We can look for traces left by the Xenohydro in the room and use them to work out when it was last here.

t_ch: …也得起来走走，换换心情……
t_en: Time to walk around and change my mood...

t_ch: 银光闪烁的舱内，留下了几根水魈本就稀疏的毛发。
t_en: In the silver cabin, there are several hairs left from the Xenohydro's sparse fur.

t_ch: 显然，在保持自由身的情况下，水魈还是会回到收容舱里睡个好觉。你不禁怀疑，它是否已经罹患斯德哥尔摩综合症。
t_en: Apparently, although it is free, the Xenohydro still returns to the Storage Zone to sleep. You can't help but wonder if it has Stockholm syndrome.

t_ch: 旁边的收容品说明已被涂黑，显示出脱逃者徒劳的愤怒和有限的反抗。
t_en: The description of the collectible item nearby has been painted over. This is a sign of anger and frustration exhibited by the escapee.

t_ch: 水魈也会联网冲浪？如此荒诞的情景是你没想到的。
t_en: Can the Xenohydro surf the Internet? You didn't expect to find such an absurd scene.

t_ch: 上面留存着「异星水魈」的研究资料，说明水魈曾在这里研究它自己。
t_en: There is research data about the Xenohydro left on the screen, showing that the Xenohydro was once here learning about itself.

t_ch: 在页面最下方键入了一行未提交的本地小字：
t_en: At the bottom of the page, there is a line of unsubmitted words:

t_ch: 「咕噜噜，一派胡言，别想抓老子！」
t_en: "*gurgle* Nonsense, you'll never catch me!"

t_ch: 毛发彻底干燥，电子屏的本地日期也停留在很久之前……
t_en: The hair is completely dry, and the date on the screen is from a long time ago

t_ch: 看样子它是不会再回来了，咱们继续找找其他地方吧。
t_en: Looks like it won't be coming back. Let's keep looking elsewhere.

t_ch: （这儿也有浓重的水腥气…但拉明娜人呢？）
t_en: (There's also a strong watery smell here... But where's Lamina?)

t_ch: （先解决这帮怪物！）
t_en: (Let me deal with these monsters first!)

t_ch: 怎么样？现在有感觉好些吗？
t_en: How's that? Are you feeling better now?

t_ch: （可恶，似乎中了水魈的陷阱！）
t_en: (Darn it, I seem to have fallen for the Xenohydro's trap!)

t_ch: （怪物的突然袭击造成了严重的伤势……）
t_en: (The monster's surprise attack caused some serious injuries...)

t_ch: {NICKNAME}！别着急，我来替你治疗。我身上留着许多药剂，本来是医疗科的粉丝送给卡波特先生的，他不稀罕，就全扔给了我。
t_en: {NICKNAME}! Don't worry, I'll treat your wounds. I have a lot of medicine on me. It was originally given to Mr. Capote by his fans among the medical staff. He didn't want it, so he gave it to me.

t_ch: 我发现这里有怪物游荡，就先躲起来了。怪我没有出声警示，才害{NICKNAME}中了水魈的陷阱。
t_en: I found a monster wandering around here, so I hid away. It's my fault that I didn't warn you. That's why you fell for the Xenohydro's trap.

t_ch: 别老是责怪自己。
t_en: Stop blaming yourself.

t_ch: 我知道了…但到目前为止，感觉我一直在帮倒忙，水魈依旧没找到。而且我也想不出其他可能的窝点了，要不然就…放弃吧？
t_en: I know... I feel like I haven't really been helping so far, and the Xenohydro is still no where to be found. And I can't think of anywhere else. So... should we give up?

t_ch: 诡计多端的水魈。
t_en: That crafty Xenohydro.

t_ch: 它一定是猜到了会有人追捕自己，才在一路上设下致命的障碍。它在暗，我们在明，这样找下去指不定还有什么危险，要不然就…放弃吧？
t_en: It must have guessed that someone is going after it, so it sets up a trap along the way. It's hidden and we're exposed. It could be more dangerous if we go ahead. So... Should we give up?

t_ch: 这么找不是办法。
t_en: We can't go on like this.

t_ch: 是我想得太简单了…费了这么大功夫，还差点遭遇危险，要不然就…放弃吧？
t_en: I underestimated the situation... We put in so much effort and almost got seriously hurt. So... should we give up?

t_ch: 是时候重新检视一回密信了。
t_en: It's time to reexamine the letter.

t_ch: 唉，兜兜转转…总之有价值的信息，应该就在「细问魈魅踪迹，偏看收容一隅。铁鞋踏破不世，待问三堂深处」这一段了，你来试着抓抓重点？
t_en: *sigh* Back to square one... Anyway, the useful information should be in the paragraph "An inquiry into spirit traces, a glance into the site of storage. Even with iron shoes worn out unearthly, one has to visit the three depths." Can you see anything useful?

t_ch: 收容一隅？
t_en: Site of storage?

t_ch: 就是收容舱段的某处，我们目前找到的两个地方，都满足这样的条件……
t_en: It's somewhere in the Storage Zone. The two places we've found so far both meet these conditions...

t_ch: 踏破不世？
t_en: Worn out unearthly?

t_ch: 简单的动宾结构，重点应该放在宾语上…等一等，这个「不世」听起来确实有些耳熟。
t_en: In this sentence structure, the focus should be on the object... Wait a minute, "unearthly" does sound familiar.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，你觉得它跟「不世之材」会有关系吗？
t_en: {NICKNAME}, do you think Unearthly Marvel has anything to do with it?

t_ch: 听起来很合理！
t_en: Makes sense!

t_ch: 嗯！我也这么觉得。所以这「铁鞋踏破不世」，指的就是需要经过由「不世之材」修建的道路吧。
t_en: Um! I think so, too. Therefore, this "iron shoes worn out unearthly" refers to passing through the road built by Unearthly Marvel.

t_ch: 那是什么东西？
t_en: What's that?

t_ch: 你不记得了吗？我听辛克尔夫人说过，你曾经用「不世之材」帮她修复过重要的道路缺损……
t_en: Don't you remember? I heard from Mrs. Hinkel that you once used Unearthly Marvel to help her repair major road defects.

t_ch: 应该是刚到空间站的时候，那时空间站新来了个「爱管闲事的年轻人」的消息还传得沸沸扬扬呢。
t_en: I think it was when you first arrived at the space station. At the time, word of a "young busybody" was spreading around the space station.

t_ch: 所以这「铁鞋踏破不世」，指的就是需要经过由「不世之材」修建的道路吧？
t_en: So this "iron shoes worn out unearthly" refers to the road built by the Unearthly Marvel?

t_ch: 三堂深处？
t_en: The three depths?
